notes: 1 Gbps copper Ethernet (IEEE 802.3ab 1999); replaced 100BASE-TX mainstream 2004-2010; supplanted by 10GbE/25GbE in datacenters
Bridging Ethernet switches; replaced hubs 1995-2000; 10/100/1000 and beyond; managed (SNMP/CLI) and unmanaged tiers
IEEE 802.11g wireless LAN ratified Jun 2003; 54 Mbps at 2.4 GHz, backward-compatible with 802.11b; mainstream 2004-2008 until 802.11n (2009)
IEEE 802.11a wireless LAN (1999); 54 Mbps at 5 GHz; less crowded spectrum than 802.11b/g; relegated to dual-band APs; supplanted by 802.11n/ac
Passing DC power over Ethernet cabling; 802.3af (2003), 802.3at PoE+ (2009), 802.3bt PoE++ (2018); ubiquitous for APs, VoIP phones, cameras, IoT
Transmission of voice over packet-switched IP networks; SIP, H.323, RTP; drove gigabit-Ethernet enterprise adoption 2004+
Gateway 7001 802.11g single-band and 7001 802.11a+g dual-band APs; Intel IPX422 processor; RADIUS, VLAN, PoE, auto-WDS; retired after Acer acquisition 2007
Broadcom-based Layer 2 managed and unmanaged Ethernet switches; 7201-16 entry $<$100; 7601-48.2 48-port gigabit $799; retired after Acer acquisition 2007
